what_it_does: Defines a static HTML design-system preview card for text inputs, a search input, checkbox-like option rows, a textarea, helper text, and a URL preview example.
observations: Includes a dsCard metadata comment with group, name, subtitle, and viewport information.
Loads shared styling from _card.css and defines file-local CSS for the card, fields, labels, helper text, inputs, search icon, checkbox visuals, and grouping.
Uses a two-column grid layout with four field areas: Hub Name, Search, Menu Options, and Meta Description.
Shows a focused input state using a gold border and soft gold box shadow.
Implements the search affordance with an inline SVG data URI background image.
Represents checked and unchecked checkbox states using span elements and CSS pseudo-elements.
Uses inline styles for muted helper text and textarea sizing/typography.
standouts: The preview covers multiple input variants and states in one compact card.
The Hub Name field includes an inline URL preview helper with highlighted URL text.
Checkbox states are visualized entirely through CSS classes and pseudo-elements.
conventions: Uses CSS custom properties for typography, colors, border radius, and surface styling.
Keeps component preview styles file-local inside a style block while relying on _card.css for shared card context.
Uses utility-like semantic class names such as field, label, help, input, search, check, box, group.
Mixes stylesheet rules with a few inline style attributes for one-off presentation details.
concerns: Checkbox examples are span-based visual controls rather than native checkbox inputs.
The text inputs do not include explicit type, name, id, or associated label attributes.
Some presentation details are embedded inline instead of being represented by reusable classes.
notes: Brief this as a static design-system specimen showing input, search, checkbox, textarea, focus, and helper-text treatments.
questions: Should the checkbox visuals correspond to real form controls in production examples?
Are the inline muted-text and textarea styles intentional exceptions or awaiting reusable classes?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated dsCard purpose matches the file: it previews inputs, checkboxes, textarea treatment, and an inline URL preview helper.
intention_signals: It seems intended to document the visual treatment and state language for ConceptV form inputs in a compact, reviewable card.
novelty: The inline URL preview helper and embedded SVG search icon make the input preview more product-specific than a generic form sample.
contribution: Contributes a token-based form-control specimen covering text input, search affordance, checkbox visual states, textarea styling, focus treatment, helper copy, and URL-preview presentation for inclusion in a fused design ledger.
summary_for_embedding: Static HTML/CSS design-system card demonstrating ConceptV input controls, search field, checkbox-like options, textarea, helper text, focus styling, and URL preview behavior.
intention_for_embedding: Created to communicate form-control styling and related helper-state patterns for a ConceptV design-system handoff preview.
